sanskrit: इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते । एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
transliteration: idaṃ śarīraṃ kaunteya kṣetram ity abhidhīyate | etad yo vetti taṃ prāhuḥ kṣetrajña iti tad-vidaḥ ||
literal_meaning: This body, O son-of-Kuntī, is called 'the field'; one who knows it is spoken of as 'the field-knower' by those who know.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes the body as an object of observation rather than identity, inviting Arjuna to shift from embodied self to witnessing awareness.
psychological_state: Beginning of metacognitive insight—seeing the body as mental content rather than the self.
trigger_scenario: A client in somatic therapy realizes 'I am not my chronic pain; I am the one noticing the pain.'
anchor_text: You are the silent observer within the body, not the body itself.
